diagnosis_summary: Acute exacerbation of congestive heart failure (CHF Stage II). Bilateral pleural effusions. Adjusted diuretic therapy with clinical improvement.
medications: Furosemide 40mg BID; Lisinopril 10mg daily; Metformin 500mg BID; Amlodipine 5mg daily
discharge_instructions: Monitor daily weight - report gain >2lbs in 24hrs. Low sodium diet (<2g/day). Fluid restriction 1.5L/day. Report worsening shortness of breath immediately.

diagnosis_summary: Unstable angina. Troponin negative x3. Cardiac catheterization showed 70% LAD stenosis. PCI with drug-eluting stent placed. Dual antiplatelet therapy initiated.
medications: ASA 81mg daily; Clopidogrel 75mg daily; Atorvastatin 80mg daily; Metoprolol 25mg BID
discharge_instructions: Take dual antiplatelet therapy without interruption. No heavy lifting >10lbs for 2 weeks. Report any chest pain immediately. Cardiac rehab referral arranged.

diagnosis_summary: Acute exacerbation of COPD with superimposed pneumonia. IV antibiotics completed. Oxygen weaning successful. CHF optimization ongoing.
medications: Tiotropium 18mcg inhaler daily; Salbutamol PRN; Prednisone taper 40-30-20-10mg over 8 days; Apixaban 5mg BID; Furosemide 20mg daily
discharge_instructions: Complete prednisone taper as prescribed. Use pulse oximeter twice daily. Report fever or worsening cough. Keep follow-up with respirology in 1 week.

diagnosis_summary: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy progression. HbA1c 8.9%. Medication adjustment. Foot care education provided. Nephrology co-management for CKD Stage 3.
medications: Metformin 1000mg BID; Gabapentin 300mg TID; Insulin Glargine 20 units at bedtime; Ramipril 5mg daily
discharge_instructions: Check blood sugar 4 times daily (fasting + before meals). Inspect feet daily for wounds. Report any new numbness or color changes. Follow up with endocrinology in 2 weeks.

diagnosis_summary: Gross hematuria evaluation. CT urogram showed 2.3cm left renal mass suspicious for RCC. Urology referral expedited. Baseline labs stable.
medications: Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily; Ramipril 5mg daily; Metformin 500mg BID
discharge_instructions: Monitor urine color - report bright red blood or clots. Stay well hydrated. Pre-op assessment scheduled. Report fever or flank pain immediately.

diagnosis_summary: Transient ischemic attack (TIA). MRI showed no acute infarct. Carotid duplex: 60% right ICA stenosis. Anticoagulation optimized. Speech therapy initiated.
medications: Apixaban 5mg BID (reduced to 2.5mg BID per age/weight); Atorvastatin 40mg daily; Amlodipine 5mg daily; Calcium + Vitamin D
discharge_instructions: Monitor for any new speech difficulty or weakness. Blood pressure checks twice daily. Fall prevention measures. Report any bleeding signs.
